reviews.comment: Professor Hamid was a wonderful teacher. Lectures were interesting and easy to understand. Professor Hamid is kind, funny, and incredibly intelligent. I would take any class he is teaching.

Professor Hamid is very direct but caring. He is the authority on Motivational Interviewing at the school. If you are new to MI, it can be intimidating when he pops into breakout rooms to assess your skills. The final was an easy role play with a partner that can be rehearsed and re-recorded before submission if needed.
He is amiable and smart but felt the class was run too much by students and there was no agenda. Gender & sexuality was not discussed as a feature of PROP.
Prof Hamid is so kind and engaging in class. A thoughtful grader. Very understanding. I would 100% take another class with Hamid.

AMAZING! would take him for all my classes if I could! He is very mindful, really there to help you learn. The way he speaks to his students makes you feel like he truly cares about your growth in the field.

Dr. Hamid is warm, empathetic, and creates a democratic and equitable classroom environment. Great teacher-you get the sense he cares more about you actually learning the skills and content than having you do assignments that are overly taxing. He's great. Will take him again if given the opportunity.
Empathy-challenged. Talks constantly, but teaches little.
Very Knowledgeable. Makes the class very interesting.
I think the class was just wasted, I didnt get enough information. he made the students talk 90% of the class.  Would not advice to take his International Social Welfare class.
Extremely knowledgeable, approachable, excellent overall

Pros: He gives basic insight to his work at AQR. He is engaging in the first few classes. Tries his best to make many points clear to students.

Cons: HW was a bit much, you have to use AI for it because there's a gap between the theory and application. His grading is very suspect. He does not make things clear.

Got a good grade but worked for it.

My team and I were able to discuss our research and strategy frameworks during interviews for quant roles in the bank and Hedge Funds. Professor Botter's class is widely regarded as one of the popular and most impactful courses at Columbia University, within the Mathematical Finance program.

Must take! An extremely well paced class with clarity. Alberto seeks feedbacks after each lecture and adjusts based on them. He makes sure everyone truly understands the concepts and how they apply in practice. You will get unique practitioners' views and genuine recruiting advice from him and guest lecturers that you won't get elsewhere.

The course is well-structured, balancing theory and practical applications. The instructor keeps students motivated, encourages participation, and is receptive to feedback. Real-world insights from speakers, group projects, and case studies enhance learning, while support for recruiting adds further value.

Professor Botter is an exceptional professor. He actively seeks feedback about his teaching and class structure and impressively improves upon it weekly. What stands out the most is his professionalism, his engaging and well-organized lectures, and his genuine commitment to student success. I feel truly lucky to have had him as a professor.

Alberto's class offers industry insights in portfolio management and a chance to present in front of AQR practitioners, which is a valuable experience for those entering the workplace.
Most importantly, the knowledge gained from this class helped me secure a portfolio management internship. Thus, choose Alberto if you're aiming for success!

Strongly recommend to take this class with Professor Alberto Botter. Professor always explained heavy topics in the easy concept. Lectures were clear and interactive. The group project is a great practice before the summer internship.

Alberto is a great Professor that blends well theory with application, often taking his practictioner's perspective into class. His teaching style is well paced, he is highly attentive to the class' needs and welcomes feedback to improve the course. Overall, taking his class was a great experience that I would repeat in a New York minute!

The professor possesses exceptional professional abilities, characterized by an inspirational teaching style and an innate talent for conveying complex material with clarity and enthusiasm. His lectures are nothing short of amazing, captivating students with engaging content and a dynamic presentation.

Professor Botter's expertise shines through in his clear and engaging communication. His lectures, enriched with practical insights from portfolio management, create a dynamic educational experience. His valuable career advice underscores a genuine commitment to student success. Highly recommended for a blend of theory and real-world expertise.

Professor Botter, a seasoned practitioner, excels in clear, engaging communication. His lectures, enriched by practical insights from portfolio management, offer a dynamic educational experience. His valuable career advice showcases a genuine commitment to student success. Highly recommended for a blend of theory and real-world expertise.

Math 5220 by Professor Botter is one of the best classes I've taken at Columbia. The lectures are amazingly organized and contains all relevant HF/Asset Management info that you are going to need. Professor Botter is always available, brought amazing connections to class, is extremely careful in organizing his lectures and patient to our questions.
I found Prof. Alberto Botter's classes to be very practical in their content. And he patiently answered our questions during the breaks of each class and after class!

Professor Alberto shows patience and kindness in answering questions. The course includes five hws, one group project(just code and presentation, without reports), and an open note exam. The course covers the basic investment strategies. Recommend this course!

Great professor, deep knowledge of the industry. What I really appreciated was his ability to use theoretical concepts and apply them to real life situations. On top of that the professor is extremely available to discuss matters outside of the class like internship, recruiting and career topics in general. He is great at explaining his material.

he is making complex topics easy to understand. And, patiently answers all questions, even those that seem simple but are essential for clarity. He also takes student feedback seriously, using it to constantly improve his lectures.

Truly a great professor. The course focuses on a lot of imperative elements when constructing, maintaining, and optimizing portfolios. Highly recommend taking his course. Very kind and always willing to help the students.

Professor Botter is an accomplished practitioner. He is a clear and effective communicator and detail-oriented.
His practical approach and experience as portfolio manager make his lectures exciting and interesting. He is always willing to help student with his career advice. His class is definitely recommended.

Alberto is one of the BEST professor I have ever met.
1.He talks clearly and reasonably.
2.He is very effective in answering questions. He can understand your questions quickly and give good answers.
3.He is very nice. He is willing to give career advices outside of classroom.
Definitely have a coffee chat with him even if you don't take his class.

Alberto is the best. His lectures are full of useful information which would be hard to acquire anywhere else. He is a well-respected practitioner and his perspectives are always enriching. I've learned a lot from him and I know I will continue to learn from him in the future.

Alberto provides one of the best practical courses in the MAFN Program. He summarized his equity investment insights into his lecture. Students got a chance of team research and presentation with Alberto's guidance and feedback. He also invited guest speakers to give students a broader view of the quant investment industry. Definitely recommend.

Professor Botter's lectures are well structured. He provides a clear syllabus and strictly follows the syllabus throughout the semester. The midterm exam was straightforward and the group project was a great experience.
In addition, Professor Botter emphasizes participation, which facilitates focus and reflection on the lectures.

Alberto Botter's class was exciting, he started by laying solid foundations before diving deeper into the subject. I enjoyed the fact that his class was theoretically solid while at the same time relevant to current industry practices. The material was always very clearly explained,  and he always tried to gather feedback to improve his course.

This professor is so supportive in and outside class. The students voluntarily come early to the class to chat with professor. The professor will bring the newest market news and views in hedge fund industry to the whole class. The things you will learn in the lectures will be so much useful and practical as the professor is a portfolio manager.

This class would be very helpful if you are interested in learning about the fundamental investment strategies in quant investment as well as the career as a quant portfolio manager. He also covers interesting topics like taxation that is usually not covered in other portfolio management classes.
It is one of my favorite classes and the most practical class in the MAFN program.

Super recommend. It is the best course that I have taken so far. Professor Botter explained really well on the process of how to construct the portfolio step by step. And we have guest lectures that bring more insight into the hedge fund industry. Take it, otherwise, you may regret it.

I genuinely enjoyed Prof. Botter's class this semester! He is an expert in the field so I learned quite a lot about investment management from a buy-side perspective in his lectures! He also cares about students' growth and frequently asks for constructive feedback! I highly recommend his class to anyone interested in taking his class!

Alberto gives detailed feedback on projects and career suggestions. Lectures are organized well by providing both theoretical knowledge
and real world examples and applications. No homework but needs efforts and active participation if you want to do well. He pays attention to every comment after each lecture. Learnt a lot through the course

Impressive class: logical and highly applied, his humor can fully ignite the atmosphere.
/ Friendly: Always willing to help, students are like friends with him.
/ Life mentor: In the coffee chat, he shared with me very useful advice on my career and valuable principles. Very lucky to be mentored by him in my school-days. Highly recommended!

Professor Botter is highly competent and explains the presented topics clearly and with passion.
He teaches from a practitioners' point of view, without neglecting the theoretical aspects of the material.
He's always available for clarifications and any kind of question as well as quickly to respond to email inquiries.
One of the most intelligent and kind professors you will meet at Columbia
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Concepts are explained with strong intuition and real-world examples, which makes even technical material much easier to understand. The professor genuinely cares about student learning and is always willing to help.

Professor Arora teaches an inspiring Applied Risk Analytics course. His diverse lectures include guest talks from world-class professionals, showcasing real-life use cases. He balances this with a rigorous analytical and mathematical approach. 
He is approachable and kind.
I love my Professor. He is very approachable.
IEORE4577 is a course that worth taking.
He is a really great professor
amazing prof on topic of risk analytics..tons os use cases and techniques learnt.
I enjoyed Professor Arora's course. He encourages students to carry out innovative models and ideas for the group project.
does not teach and have guest lecturers teach every week. Limited  structure of class. No consistent or transparent grading scale. Did not learn anything
An exceptional person who offers a fresh perspective on teaching. I'd be thrilled to learn from him again.
A standout person who excels at connecting with others. His innovative teaching methods make him a standout educator.
Easy A, great instructor!

Great professor, he does a great job getting guest lectures from top folk in the healthcare industry. Loved his interjection of guest lectures as it added variety in the class. You get out what you put in from this class, and the professor is open to introducing you to internship and employment opportunities, and giving career advice/guidance.

Amit is one of my favorite profs at CU. Very intriguing and focuses on getting real-world learning experience rather than how well you can test. Amit really cares about each student and wants you to succeed. Very accessible, connects us to industry professionals and research opportunities. Cannot recommend this class enough.

Great course if you're interested in the healthcare industry. Lots of case studies, and guest speakers. Participation are mainly attendance & inclass quiz. A final group project at the end that counts towards 50% of the grade. Professor was a nice guy, would be happy to give you course help or career advise.
Fantastic class with plenty of guest lectures from highly successful people in the field and the professor goes above and beyond to help students out.
Absolute icon, so intelligent and so wonderful to have as a professor. assignments are easy to follow but they make you engage with the city in a way that most professors dont utilize.

I am not at CSSW anymore, I graduated 6 years ago! I loved learning with and from professor seals. She really changed my life and how I think about using my time and valuing it. 
I am now PhD student & teaching. I love teaching. One thing prof.Seals said during class that has stuck with me until this day is: " You have to know what gives you life."
Love her as a person, very easy assignments and easy grader. Did not learn much, unfortunately. If you want to be challenged and absorb the material then I recommend another professor.

I absolutely adored Professor Seals. She is very straightforward about her expectations and only requires the main group projects and final exam, no busy work or pointless discussion posts. She did an extensive review session for the final and made the material easy to understand. I'm stoked to take her again for Advocacy next semester!
Wonderful professor, few assignments and a easy grader
Changed my life
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I took professor Seals for advocacy and mass incarceration and she is one of the best professors Ive ever taken. She is so open to answering questions and the classes are very engaging. I definitely recommend taking her.

Fantastic, caring, empathetic professor. Professor Seals fostered a discussion-based dynamic in the classroom, encouraging students to share openly and build on readings and class material. She was also incredibly available via communication. I would highly recommend her for Mass Incarceration or any other class - she is a gem.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: She encouraged us during class time to debrief about a racially-motivated incident of violence. I am a member of the targeted racial class. I stated in the chat that I was upset and didn't wish to say more. Professor Werman almost immediately called me out and asked me to "say more" about how I felt. I refused. She is not trauma-informed and harms

I was shocked to see such terrible reviews for Professor Werman who, aguably, was the best professor I had at Columbia. She was one of the only professors to bring a clear clinical perspective and based in concrete social work practices. She was clinically practical and did WONDERS to my therapy practice. It was the only class I was excited for.

Amy is my favorite professor at CSSW. Read between the lines of the denigrating reviews. They were written by a type of CSSW student who is overly sensitive, radical, and barely functional. If you want to learn how to be a solid, sound psychotherapist, Amy is a must-take at Columbia. *I would have preferred less group work. Still a 5/5 class.

She takes everything overly seriously, reporting minor issues like laptop use or lateness to the advising center. She's rude, and extremely strict with grading, allowing no make-ups. Some behavior raises suspicions of racism.  

Her ungrading system lets students grade themselves, but she then argues over scores with you. 

Not recommended.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: AVOID! She really taught me the lesson that I should "RMP" before I enroll the classes. She doesn't care about students. She was very strict with grading and didn't allow any make up. She responds to emails very rudely. When she walks up to you with a worried look on her face, slowly asks "Are You OK?", you wonder if you really look terrible.

Awful. Although she does provide good feedback on assignments which I really appreciated it, she is not clear on assignments/due dates and when you ask questions she is very passive aggressive and rude. Doesn't care about students

Amy Werman is one of the most oppressive professors Ive met. She claims to be anti-oppressive yet she is openly classist, ableist, and racist. She is extremely defensive, and despite asking students to be candid she refuses to be held accountable. Her grading is unclear and she gives contradictory information when asked questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: She emphasizes the importance of participation during the class. Given that this was an online class she wanted students to be in chat the entire time. This took away from taking notes and simply being in the moment learning. She often reached out via email to those students who were not always in chat. Overall, not a fan of the teaching style.

Professor Werman is a terrible professor. She is incredibly disrespectful and passive aggressive towards her students. Does not teach well. Content is boring and rigid. Genuinely just a bad person and a bad professor. Avoid her classes at all costs.

reviews.comment: I have never had such an awful experience. She used this system called ungrading where students grade themselves but she argues with the students about what grade they have given. As a social worker, it is crucial to understand that people are navigating various life situations. She has zero empathy for these struggles. Never take her class.

reviews.comment: TERRIBLE INSTRUCTOR! DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS! SHE IS A REALLY TOUGH GRADER AND REQUIRES HEAVY PARTICIPATION.

Prof Werman took over my class half way through due to COVID-19 complications. She is a good teacher, though sometimes she does things last minute like emailing the class to read something 1-2 days before the next class. She also tends to not explain assignments as well as I'd like - she's also somewhat of a tough grader. Participation matters!!!!!

reviews.comment: The teaching was very unstructured and not objective. The material was challenging but the class session weren't used to discuss the content. The teaching was very disorganization and not helpful to my learning.

Professor Werman comes to class prepared to make research a comprehensible and applicable subject to social work. She is enthusiastic, clear, and engaging. She welcomes questions and class participation; always welcoming different opinions and happy to re-explain concepts students have difficulty grasping.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Werman is very nice. However, do not confuse this with easiness. She is a strict grader and is very particular about structure and grammar. There are no make-ups or rewrites. BUT if you take the comments of the paper seriously and show significant improvement, she takes that into consideration and you will be absolutely fine.

AVOID! This class is easy but Prof. Werman's teaching style is really confusing and she often contradicts herself and changes assignment details at the last minute. Also, she's a stickler for in class electronics and treats the students like it's high school all over again. I wish I had taken the waiver exam.

I learned so much in this class due to the teaching method she used in class. you will walk out of the class learning what  came in for. She is attenive to her stuents and accessible after class time. She is a fair grader, clear and concise. I would recommend her for this course.
Learned so much in this class due to teaching style of this professor. Class was always engaged. Comments on assignments were always helpful.

Easy and mandatory class. Class would be better if professor is more knowledgeable about more practical and reality in the field. A lot of the materials are undergrad level when this was a Master's concentration class. Def could be way better, but I can see how many students didn't mind since the class was easy.

Professor Akilova teaches policy and i have learned a lot this semester, which changed my perspective on policy. The discussion in class was not always easy, but she facilitated it well. I like that the classes are not always lecture-heavy. the assignments have also contributed a lot to my learning. she is approachable
horrible and uncredible professor. poor quality and brings down the reputation of columbia.

While she is very kind & knowledgable when it comes to policy, her lectures were incredibly dry, unclear & not informative. She'd have powerpoints with 100 slides, but we'd only get through 10; she's not a great facilitator so class discussions were all over the place & too long. I don't feel I actually learned many concrete concepts & skills.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GROUP PROJECTS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment:
Prof. Homayoon was really helpful during and post lectures as well as very accomodating of our project ideas. The insights given by him during the lectures as well as project discussion meetings were immensely useful! 
Do take his class!

In my final project, Prof. Beigi met with me 1:1 to discuss my proposal and gave targeted, practical advice. Throughout the project, his guidance was consistently effective—an outstanding advisor. He's also the fastest professor I've ever seen at replying to emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great course that builds the core math for advanced ML, with helpful signals/control connections. Teaching is clear and concept-driven. Weekly/biweekly problem sets (mainly until midterms) reinforce lectures. Final project: pick one ML problem from a large list and explore in depth. Fair grading; ~6–10 hrs/week.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Beigi's speech recognition course is very technical and rewarding. The open-ended projects are very engaging, and classmates' presentations are great to learn from. I discovered my interest in research through this course. Office hours are very helpful and highly recommended.

I took Prof. Beigi's maths course in Fall 2024 and found it very inspiring. Prof. Beigi is so knowledgeable and supportive. This course stood out from other ML courses I took by covering materials in various topics like measure theory, signal processing, information theory etc. It's rare to find a course that brings all of these areas together!

Even if you are not familiar with control, that's completely fine—he begins the course with the fundamental mathematics needed to understand the material. He is very open to students and truly cares about each person in the class. When you ask a question, he is always happy to explain further and makes sure you fully understand the concept.

Professor Beigi is an excellent professor, and clearly very knowledgeable and passionate about his craft. He is also one of the most approachable and kind professors I have taken a class with at Columbia. He makes the challenging material very comprehensive, and spends a good amount of time building a foundational math background at the beginning.

Professor Beigi really knows his stuff and is always willing to answer questions. His vast knowledge in the subject matter becomes clear from the first lecture. This course was fast-paced but rewarding. Only an expert like Professor Beigi could teach such a course at a high level. Lectures and assignments were engaging and non-standard.

Professor Beigi is amazing and his passion and enthusiasm for teaching make for a great course environment. I most appreciated Professor Beigi's emphasis on how topics across different subfields are interconnected (from linear algebra and information theory to partial differential equations, optimization theory and deep learning).

An absolute legend on Speech Recognition. He had his own textbook and company. He replies to emails in minutes. It's fun to discuss any topics with him. Start the project early if you want to take Speech Recognition because it takes time to train the model.

Extremely thorough but moves through mathematical topics quickly. Although it was initially overwhelming, I realized it was for future reference in terms of all the underlying math behind machine learning. The project was incredibly creative, and I got to do work that mattered. Challenging exam and neural network MATLAB problem, but it is mathy!

The most important class I've taken at Columbia. This course rigorously highlights the beauty and power of math. Prof. Beigi carefully curated the selection of topics that are essential for understanding deep learning and signal processing. He is undeniably a leading expert in the field and an invaluable mentor to have with his exceeding kindness.

Prof. Beigi's depth of knowledge is truly impressive and he is able to convey it to the class in a very clear and engaging. He is always ready to answer questions and provide additional explanations, and is always enthusiastic about the subject. I would highly recommend this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Strongly encourage this course for all serious STEM students. Never seen a more comprehensive & detailed course offered anywhere. You will develop a much deeper math intuition. Dr. Beigi is caring & very knowledgable. Ignore any bad reviews (Matlab was used for one assignment to teach the "plumbing" of NNs, not use of pre-built python functions).

Professor Beigi is highly knowledgeable and inspirational. The course is a perfect amalgamation of learning concepts and applying them via a hands on project! Professor is very approachable and solves individual student queries. Highly Recommended!

Prof. Beigi is highly knowledgeable & explains each & every concept in-depth. He is well-versed in the topics & takes efforts in explaining the reasoning & the maths behind working of ML models & Signal recognition concepts. The class is essential for everyone who are looking to understand core concepts behind use of neural network / ML algorithms!

I initially took this class based on the excellent reviews I had seen here. It left a lot to be desired. The prof is knowledgeable but mediocre at transmitting information. He blitzed through the course content without explaining much. Very old school, for instance by forcing us to use MATLAB instead of other languages. Little guidance for project.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: good

I don't have a strong background in Info theory and math, so I have to study hard (really hard) for that part of the material. Luckily, Professor Beigi is super knowledgable; his lecture and book cover everything in detail. The knowledge covered by the course is about three times more than regular course. You will learn A LOT if you want to.

Prof. Beigi is by far one of the most inspirational people you can hope to come across. He teaches the course with passion & genuinely cares about advancing the knowledge of students. Morn, night, holiday or not, he works tirelessly to answer queries. I highly recommend his lectures. Also, he truly cares that you do well in life. A gem of a person!

Professor Beigi is one of the smartest and most approachable professors I have met at Columbia. He genuinely wants his students to learn, and his passion for speech recog is apparent. His office hours are strongly attended as he sits with students one-on-one to make sure theyre on track. He is a very well-liked and respected professor on campus.

Heard of walking-talking encyclopedia? This is him! He was THE most knowledgeable professor I have seen in Columbia. From signal processing to machine learning to NLP - he's got it all in his head. Lemme demonstrate how helpful he is: He would take your laptop to save your life and fix your problems AND explain what went wrong.  Respect!

Professor Homayoon Beigi had the patience to explain key concepts  and guide me throughout the semester. The content was really intimidating at first  but I  started to understand key concepts as the semester progressed. This was one of the best classes with a mix of both practice and theory. He has real passion for the subject area.  Rare in sch

Professor Beigi is the most brilliant professor I have had so far!  He is extremely knowledgeable on many different topics, which is great for such a multidisciplinary course.  He is very approachable and unbelievably kind, with great drive to make sure everyone understands every bit of material.  His textbook is superb and a great reference.

As a grad student who has switched my major/school twice, and while having interacted with many student peers, many in his course, I can only convey the real sense of appreciation we all have toward his Witt,intellect, multi-levels of knowledge.. from fundamental sciences to engineering &entrepreneurship.     super bright & one of a kind indeed!

I haven't met many professors here or elsewhere who take a genuine interest in their students, which is honestly why I remain enrolled. It's one of the few courses where the professor's enthusiasm and passion for the subject matter was contagious.
very demanding

I would not recommend taking this class. The pro is demanding and assigns tremendous work to grads. The course is complicated, and the workload feels like it's four times heavier than my other graduate classes. If you somehow find yourself bored in New York and want something to fully occupy your time, this class will definitely do the job.

How can someone cram a marketing class with so many meaningless, trivial, time-wasting tasks just in one semester? And then when grading don't say why were the points deducted and what's the criteria? (*^_^*) If you will choose this class, good luck to you (*^_^*)

Prof Boston is an amazing professor in many ways, but turns into a different person when triggered. Her class was originally one of my favorite classes. She makes herself out to be the victim, doesn't take accountability for how she contributes to a conflict and retaliates. I learned a lot in the class but hope students never experience what I did.

Professor Boston is fantastic. I felt like I left with a wealth of information and affirmed in why I want to do this work. Professor Boston is thoughtful,funny and kind. She gives great feedback and she's approachable. I would recommend her classes to any one
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor continuously sought to alienate students in different ways, whether that be by supporting and uplifting some or degrading others. She has many good ideas and lectures but these were overshadowed by her continuous unprofessionalism. I do not suggest taking her.

This professor is extremely unprofessional. She pointed out and named two students whom she felt "supported by" and was extremely rude and dismissive to the students who respectfully asked for clarification throughout the semester  . More than half of the class went to administration to complain that she was grading with bias.

Professor Boston goes through community guidelines every week and she states "feelings are not facts" yet she brought her personal feelings into the class. Sent the class an email stating that students should start writing like "Columbian students" and that she is surprised many students got into Columbia with the way the assignments are written.

Professor Boston challenged me to lean into discomfort and encouraged me to think beyond what my environment has lead me to believe thus far.  She is REAL with her students and for that reason a lot of people really struggle in her course.  Very different than any other course experience, but such a gift!

Professor Boston is an amazing professor, I would recommend that everyone take her at least once if they get the chance. She is interactive with her students and creates a classroom enviornment that is a safe and brave space for all who enter. She is challenging and gives great feedback, you will leave her class with a new perspective on life.

Dr. Cogburn is incredibly bright, interesting, and pleasant. Her course is great and she teaches in a really engaging way, but you will be graded more harshly than peers in other sections of this course. It's a trade-off, because she really is so great to learn from and listen to, but it comes at a price.

absolutely do not miss a reading you will regret it!!! it will be needed for your final paper!!!!  definitely show up and engage! tough grader for sure, but she means well and is a great sweet person.
She is cool and amazing. The lectures were interesting and high-quality, the content was very cutting-edge. Not a lot of assignments. High level of freedom for readings and assignments.

Prof. Cogburn taught the first module of this 3-module 1-semester course. She provided high-quality lectures and fostered meaningful whole-group discussions. The module she led was based around race, which is an interesting topic, but one that can be frustrating without proper facilitation. I would love to take another course with Prof. Cogburn.
Overall, this was a positive experience with Professor Clinton. Yes, she can be a little rigid and is a tough grader but shes probably the smartest professor Ive ever learned from. Would recommend
This teacher was the absolute worst ive ever had the deprivilege of attending , i would never in a million years recommend this course to not even my worst enemies.

reviews.comment: really amazing professor!
Her class is okay. Although she seems never to get over how she lost a presidential election or something
She is nice
Very intelligent and insightful
Completely bias and opinion based content

Professor Clinton delivers content effectively with a focus on real-world applications. Her classes provide a comprehensive understanding of political history and policy, fostering a conducive learning environment. Recommended for those seeking a structured and informative approach to the subject.
slay prof I rec.

If you want random group project grades despite top assignment scores, take his class. Ensure he remembers your name, or he might dock points for assumed absences. Choose teammates wisely, as your final grade hinges entirely on the project.
He is the most caring Professor. One only needs to reach out to him and he ensures the TAs also pay special attention to those questions for the rest of class. If I could attend his classes, I would.
Good person, bad teacher. The professor has no idea how to teach this course. You will learn nothing from his course. Worst course I have taken at CU.

His lectures are extremely boring and he has no idea about managing his TAs. Project deadlines and specifications change all the time. TAs procrastinate and Professor Lin does nothing about their incompetence. This class is a waste of money since you can learn all the material in your own time using one of cloudera or ibm tutorials.

I submitted a formal complaint about disparate treatment. The Dean told me it would take him 1-2 years to investigate. At a journalism school! Who can wait that long to graduate? Her behavior is really a reflection of the school.

While Prof. Cross is held in high regard, her teaching style will not work for many students. The condescending tone comes from a certain type of workplace environment that students who graduate the program may fall into. Do not be fooled.. journalism is home to many kind, creative, supportive, team-focused environments. In 2020s we deserve better.

I agree with the majority of the comments: Cross is rude, condescending and unprofessional. I came into the program with a graduate degree in another discipline. This is important because I have a lot of experience with professors and she was by far the worst I've ever had. She is so wrapped up in her own projects she could care less and it shows.

According to this "professor", if you're thick-skinned, an extroverted bully who knows how to gossip well-enough, then you're a good journalist. All these attributes are whats wrong with journalism in America today. Being rude - even if it's used as a shield - never makes a journalist real. If you want to be one like the rest, take her class

As many have said above- while she holds students to high standards, she is extremely condescending, rude, and unprofessional. While she does push you to achieve, it is more driven out of fear of being made fun of, talked down to, or screamed at (yes that happens, obscenities are usually involved). Really inappropriate for an adult and teacher.

Unprofessional and disrespectful. Producing a documentary about her life doesn't make her experienced and producing another with a frontline team does not give her a green light to abuse her students. While I took her class last year, I still can't get over how little I learned from her. I don't recommend this teacher.

Cross's irrational moody disrespectful attitude over shadows the very little wisdom or knowledge she may show sporadically during the semester. If you're paying a fortune to be in this school, don't waste your money on any of her classes.

Prof. Cross is a no-nonsense professor who holds her students up to the highest standards... as she should.  Having come into her class with prior experience, I found her teaching methods helpful, but not sure how those new to the game were able to keep up.  Worth taking her class if you are dedicated to becoming a better journalist.

Really undedicated, insensitive to the fact the students aren't professionals yet, can't be bothered to help because she's too caught up in her own projects. Very mean spirited too. A total nightmare and it's too bad because so many people want to learn about documentary and she's a horrible teacher.

horrible, rude, condescending, arrogant

Not very friendly for office hours.

One of the best professor's I've ever had! Thorough, caring, goes through tremendous amounts of effort to make sure students comprehensively know the material. would not recommend

This course was the highlight of my visiting student experience at Columbia. Professor Sahakyan's lectures were eye-opening, multi-faceted, and overall fascinating. I learned a lot about the politics and economics of international cooperation. A HUGE bonus point: he gives valuable career advice.
He's really intelligent and easygoing, I wish I could take him a third time. Probably my favorite professor at Fordham.

Davit is inspiring, approachable, funny, and very knowledgeable. 
The course is a great introduction to international political economy with a focus on international economic cooperation. The workload is average compared to other polisci courses at Columbia.

Davit's Advances in IPE seminar was the most amazing seminar class I have taken at Columbia. As someone who doesn't have a strong background in economics, I had the most rewarding experience of understanding the intricacies of IPE through a real-world-related and unbiased perspective.
He is such a good professor, breaks down complex topics really well. His lectures are really interesting and helps you become more aware of globalization. Definitely take this class!
Dr Davit is kind, understanding, humorous, and extremely intelligent. His lectures are funny, effective, and inspiring. One of my favorite professors at Columbia.
Davit is great at explaining complex econ topics in the most accessible way! One of the best professors at Columbia!

Dr. Sahakyan is extremely intelligent and very approachable. He is very open minded and will go out of his way to make sure his personal opinions do not overwhelm class discussions. He is extremely good at explaining things, which is especially important for students who do not have a strong background in economics.

Professor Sahakyan is amazing. His lectures are clear and very interesting. He provides a thorough and comprehensive account of globalization and spices it all up with occasional tangents on history, philosophy, and literature. I absolutely recommend his course.

This was one of the most interesting courses I have taken at Columbia. Dr. Sahakyan is patient, engaging, friendly, and very effective professor. He is especially good at simplifying complex ideas and making the easy to comprehend.

Davit's class was very well organized and easy to follow. He is especially good at explaining complex economic topics, making them easy for non-economists to understand. Davit is motivational and gives valuable career advice.

Professor Shilane is wonderful, resourceful and infinitely helpful to his students. THE WORST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER HAD, disrespectful to students and unhelpful

You need to spend a lot time to go through the reading, lectures notes before the class. Otherwise you will not able to understand what he's saying.

I burned my fingers last semester and could hardly type to finish the final exam. Prof. Shilane and my caring academic advisor Katie offered great help during that difficult period. Thank you so much professor!

He is the most responsible instructor that I have ever met. He goes above and beyond to help out and is very reasonable when it comes to grading. Really engaging professor. Has a lot of passion for his teaching and is happy to help students.
makes simple concepts confusing. The class is a mandatory class and if it was not many people not to take his class. Hes just shouting andis ESL no one can understand his  saying

Great professor but somewhat difficult to follow his thought process (because he is that brilliant). The class is a tough one so be prepared to work, work, work. He is very easy to approach and really wants his students to do well so that's a plus!

He is absolutely EXCELLENT. There is no better teacher. It shows that he cares about your learning, he plans very throughly what and HOW he is going to say. Explains everything with passion and truly wants you to learn.And he is very intelligent and FUNNY!
I liked his class generally. A little bit hard but he is very enthusiastic person. He can teach..
very hard teacher, not enough clarity, and eplanations
Brilliant professor, dynamic lecturer, nice person, and made me remember how much I love learning. After taking her AI art history course I have decided to apply to graduate school.

Prof. Spratt is a wonderful teacher and deeply inspiring person. I have taken two courses with Prof. Spratt, ArtHum and AI art history, which were very different classes, but both excellent. Solid professor. I realized art history is actually very interesting and challenging in a good way.

Best class you can take at Columbia if you actually want to learn something. Prof Spratt is so knowledgeable and nice. Brilliant professor--I learned more in this class than any other CU class. Professor Spratt is probably the best professor I've had at Columbia. This is a class that you learn so much in. I really felt like I grew as a student from the feedback she gave me. His makes excellent suggestions on narrative journalism and my writing improved, but his anger gets in the way.

Freedman is phenomenal.  During my dual-degree at Columbia, I?ve never had a professor as dedicated to improving his students? writing and diligent about editing their work as Freedman.  He has a genuine reverence for journalism and inculcates his students with exemplary habits. I only regret that I was too timid to go to his office hours more.

Prof. Freedman is a little rough around the edges in terms of personal style, but if you look beyond that, he cares passionately about journalism, his students and the value of integrity.  He never came to lecture less than 110 percent prepared and what he offered was from the heart.

I was in Freedman's book class. It's one of the best at J-school. It required super-human work and, for me, no spring break. He could be blunt. But it's rediculous to expect him to shower you with complements. His comments were comprehensive and helpful, and he did commend strivers. After graduation he also put me in touch with a book agent.

I had Freedmen for his book class in 05. His "sophistic dialectic" helped me get an agent the day after graduation, and now i'm on a book contract. Plus, you used "byzantine" wrong, you nitwit. Dr. McIlvain gives the most amazing, concise, and detailed lectures. Her ability to simplify complex topics and build them back up is incredible. She is also incredibly caring and easy to reach outside of class!

SHE IS SO AMAZING! I learned so much!!! She truly cares about your success in class.

favorite professor!

awesome professor!!

dr. mia was an incredibly thoughtful, caring, intelligent professor. she clearly put a lot of thought into our course and gave insightful feedback on our assignments. i learned so much and i would highly recommend her! thank you for everything

its good but difficult for second language people to understand
She is an expert in this.

Professor is extremely knowledgeable in the course material and breaks it down in a way that is easy to understand. She is great at explaining core concepts while also is able to answer very detailed questions on the subject matters. Great Professor.

Have taken Professor Hanlon a few times now, great teacher. Extremely knowledgable.

great work

Professor is very knowledgable and is great at explaining difficult topics.

clear and concise conceptual lessons, could be a little more thorough explaining relationships between systems and WHY things are the way they are and the application of the concept.
