I thought the class material provided was very helpfule. All the tips and tricks really helped me! The only thing that felt was lacking was time, we ran through it pretty quickly. Otherwise I am extremely satisfied with the prof.
very motivated and driven; style is very different from other classes in the university; much more art based than architecture; not the greatest listener and a little limiting, but very supportive.
Umber was extremely helpful and clear; I would recommend her to anyone.
Exposed me to a different side of architectural design that is not commonly taught at the university.  It was an extremely unique, creative and enjoyable course.

Extremely informative.  Might schedule the class with more breaks and more time to help absorb the material a little easier. Hard to listen take notes and absorb the information at the rate she is delivering the class.
Well organized and helpful. I would take a course from her again. Nice legs...
Great help with the somewhat quirky graphic exams-both Site Planning and Building Technology seminars for ARE were very helpful.
She's is the best instructor i've had for AIA seminars and even most of my college courses.  hope to learn more from her.

took her class for the CSE Oral Exam (for the CA. Architects License) and she did a good job covering the material.  very much to the point, there is not a moment wasted in the class.  the class was fast but well worth it.

She is very knowledgeable and to the point.  Wastes no time.  Material was presented clearly and comprehensively.  I recommend her highly for those who are studying for Lateral Forces.  The only thing I would suggest is to to slow down the speed a little bit so that everybody's up to speed with the teaching.

THE SEMINARS WERE QUITE GOOD.  UNFORTUNATELY THERE WAS ONE EXAM I DID NOT PASS (GENERAL STRUCTURES) BUT I DO KNOW THAT WAS MY OWN FAULT DUE TO NOT BEING ABLE TO KEEP UP WITH THE STRUCTURED STUDY GUIDE.  ALL OF THE INFORMATION GIVEN WAS WELL PRESENTED AND EFFIENCENT.  THANKS AGAIN FOR ALL OF YOUR HELP!
Professional, knowledgeable and clear.  All round an excellent teacher.
Very useful information.  I have not yet taken the exam.  I wish the class was not as short though.... because it feels as if we are rushing through the entire class....

It wasn't what I expected... some people wanted the subject matter slower... I wanted it faster.  I was bored, there was a lot of repetitive information and most of the subject matter were items that I had been using for years... but it not a reflection on the teacher.  She went over exactly what she was supposed... I just couldn't use much of it.

Professor was extremely detailed and effecient.  Our class section was changed over from a second teacher who felt he could not teach such a large class and it was apparent midway in the quarter he couldn't handle a small sized class as they fell from a week to two weeks behind our class. Prof.Umber was amazing and helpful-Hope she's back next qtr!
Decent teacher. Certianly knows her stuff, but goes through information very fast so make sure you pay attention.
Easy to understand and knows how to explain things well. Sometime she explains things well that we don't need to ask her the same questions again, which is good.
The instructor was knowledgable, but breezed through the material.
UMBER IS A VERY GOOD TEACHER.SHE IS AN INSPIRATION FOR ME TO GET MY LICENSE IN CALIFORNIA AFTER I READ HER RESUME AND ALL THE WORK SHE HAS PERFORMED.CONGRATULATIONS.
she is very easy to understand and she shows a lot  of interest in the subject.
Umber teaches in a manner that is very easy to understand. She works fast and makes the time go by quickly.  I understood everything she was layin' down
Lateral Forces. The teacher did a good job with the time provided. Unfortunately the time ran out without completing the mock exams or questions and answers.
Umber is gifted on her feet.  She's thinks fast and expresses things clearly.  I passed all six exams I took after her class.
Very Knowledgable and Helpful in regard to understanding what is expected to pass the ARE. Highly recommended for anyone in the process of taking the exams!
Thanks for all the help
she was great! Nice clean lay-out of the program, even the structure class was more digestable after her course & I haven't taken a math class in about 12 yrs. Thanks

The classes are very useful in providing a schedule and outline for studying.  I have passed each exam to this point following the schedule (6 total.)  The classes gave me the motivation to finish my exams efficiently, much better than I was doing on my own.  Umber is very willing to assist you when things are unclear and provides a clear outline.

i took the M/E as well as 2 of the graphics classes.  i took the M/E exam shortly after.  i felt the class was beneficial to provide just another outlet for information and reference.  the graphics classes were also helpful but were slightly rushed and could stand to be longer.
The Building Technology seminar should be for 4-5 hours instead of the 3 hour seminar to cover the content in detail
Very helpful. Tough schedule to compete with at 1 test per week. But very helful especially for structures tests.
Very thorough. I definitely felt more confident and prepared for this exam (Site Planning) as compared to others. Thank you for all your help, and I will be signing up for future courses.
The information was very helpful
Umber’s superior understanding of the material helped me to learn with greater ease and enjoyment.
Very knowledgable in the subject and can teach it very well unlike most other teachers at NewSchool
Clear and in-depth presentation of materials, but goes a little quick at times.
She is simply one of the best.
A very clear teaching style.  Her Study sessions were better then the class.
Umber has a very concise method, a genuine passion for teaching and is truly interested in the development of her students.
Umber is one of the best professors at the NSAD.  She explains the subject until everybody in class understands.  She is very helpful outside of class too. Thank you!
A well respected professor all around.  Encourages students to go forth with what she teaches.
On top of her game.  She is not only smart but a very good teacher as well.
Not only is she hot, but she's extremely smart, patient, and great at communicating.
very communicative

Ubmer was so helpful, I did not really understand a thing at the beginning but the handouts and the extra help outside of class was amazing.  she had a busy schedule and still found time to see us if we need her which all of us did. She would be wonderful addition to any school.
very well organized and professional, provided us with the tools needed to comprehend a difficult subject mater
No Comments

Absolutely awful experience taking Foundations of SW with Prof Hara, he told the class that he was afraid of conflict when he let a student say multiple racial slurs during his open mic...thoroughly disappointed with the way he handled this situation and how unsafe he made his students feel when he asked us to our classmate out ourselves.

Flexible & willing to accommodate (re: deadline extensions). Gives thoughtful & insightful feedback; cares about his student's work & their learning! A long-standing professor at CSSW, Hara is incredibly knowledgeable & acutely sensitive to the partnership between policy & social work. Lecture integrated with videos & class/group discussions.

Hara's passion for the topic shined through, and despite the constant changes and difficulties through the semester, he seamlessly adapted. I appreciate his mix of lectures, videos, and interactive activities. I've never had a professor give such in-depth feedback, you can tell he genuinely cares about his students and their work.

Richard Hara is, by far, one of the best professors I've had in my academic career. It is rare to see a professor who actively reflects on so much of his work while also getting so much out of the curriculum. Prof. Hara is one of those people I would pay to get coffee with and listen to anything he wants to say. TAKE HIS CLASS!

Best professor I've had at CSSW thus far. Incredibly kind and smart, and often quite funny. His lectures can be a bit dry at times, but I found there was always something to be learned from whatever Dr. Hara was saying. He seemed to carefully integrate feedback from students into the course, and made sure what we were learning was useful.
One of my favorite professors in the Columbia school of social work program! Do yourself a favor and take him, especially for health care policy!
Such a great professor!!! I am happy I took him after hearing negative feedback about other professors in the health care policy section.

I seriously lucked out by taking Professor Hara for health care policy! He fostered a welcoming classroom environment, which allowed for interactive learning process. He was also very approachable and understanding, basically everything you would want in a social work professor. He as well gives you all the resources you need to succeed, take him!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Caring

reviews.comment: I am so happy I took Professor Hara for my health care policy class!! He made the subject of policy easy to comprehend and his assignments/readings were interesting and relevant to the current sociopolitical climate. He was as well very understanding and clear in his grading requirements, do yourself a favor and take him!!

Dr. Hara is knowledgeable and extremely thoughtful. His teaching style is collaborative and he is extremely fair in grading. I learned so much form the class and his personal experiences. I loved the class!

Professor Hara is extremely thoughtful, well-spoken and attentive to student needs.  His approach is though-provoking and collaborative as he encourages students to provide input and direct class conversation through their contributions.  Professor Hara is attuned to the finer points of social work such as psychodynamics, use of self, ethics, etc.

While Prof. Hara is nice and means well, his classes are extremely boring and dry. We spent the entire time on our laptops looking at Facebook. He doesn't have enough variety in his lectures. That said, he's a pretty easy grader.
Her classes are pretty chill, no pressure.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Isacoff is hands down one of the best, if not the best, professor in the Psychology Department. Her teaching style is inclusive and interdisciplinary, regardless of whether you take her lecture or various seminars. She actually cares about the person behind the student, looking for ways in which each individual can thrive in the classroom.

Strange experience. So she was completely fine the whole semester, engaging lectures, very structured course, nothing to complain, seemingly clear and organized grading criteria--until the final. She said she would be "VERY lenient on the term paper and not to worry," and then I get a B. She said the paper was unclear. And I've written many papers.

Nora Isacoff is the BEST professor. She is so knowledgable, engaging, funny, and understanding. I learned so much from her and was so excited every time I got to go to her class! 100% going to try to take another course with her.
I was disturbed to witness Ivanoff repeatedly target and discriminate against Jewish students throughout the semester.

I had Dr. Ivanoff almost 20 years ago and I must say that I was challenged beyond what I was prepared for. She was my anchor professor for clinical practice and to this day as I am in my own practice and teach myself I still think back to the days when graduate students actually worked for their grades. It's sad to see such negative feedback.

Do yourself a favor: avoid completely or at minimum take it pass/fail. Get ready for a semester of anxiety and uncertainty and helpless frustration. Andre is sweet but barely teaches and throws curveballs all the time. This class had sooo much potential but turned out to be a huge let-down by class three. Major missed opportunity.

Final examination tomorrow and still have not gotten grades back on any assignment for the whole semester. Everyone feels totally unprepared. The class was so great in the beginning but turned out to be a complete disaster. Disorganized. Chaotic. Disappointing.

This is the best Professor I have ever had. She is very knowledgeable and is committed to having her students become excellent therapists. You gain the therapeutic techniques to be an excellent therapist and you understand how to treat clients with cooccurring disorders. I would not be prepared for my career if it were not for this Professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: I found this the most challenging and best class I've taken at Columbia.  Not for those who didn't come here to learn.  Prof is all in.
There's a difference between being challenged, and then when work is impossible. Worst class I have ever taken. Avoid like the plague. So stressful. High anxiety!! Ahh

I just don't understand how someone can actually give the amount of work that we have. "Several, several hours" of reading (like I read everything? LOL). It's just not even possible!!! Also not learning anything because I have a teacher that it just using her syllabus. But this teacher (Carville) is so much more sympathetic at least. Just AVOID!!

Insdorf is great lecturer, is kind when you go up to her and gets her class invited to all kinds of exclusive screenings. Her curriculum tends to be mixed: filled with some great work and some terrible work. She also can be a tad bit pretentious.

She is an amazing teacher.  She clearly has three loves: films, students, and sharing the love of film to students.  Her energy and enthusiasm was contagious brimming with knowledge and articulateness.  She always brought all of herself to the classroom.  Like it or not you knew what she loved and stood for.  That is more than I can say about most.
Professor Insdorf is articulate, insightful,  and frequently inspiring. She seems to love nothing more than sparking creative discussion. Her class was a pleasure.
The most passionate and committed professor I have ever had. I could listen to her lecture forever.
Absurdly obsessed with grammar.
Hands down the dullest, most boring lecturer I ever encountered.  Sucked all the fun out of the subject.
A great teacher and a fun class. There is no pretending about it: she knows all the people she claims to know.

Insdorf doesn't "pretend" to know anyone. She knows her directors and she knows film. Her lectures may be pre-written, but the knowledge she has is vast.  She hosts Cannes on IFC every year, and always makes Ebert look like he's never seen a film (or director) in his life.

Sometimes, she likes to pretend that she was actually present at important historical events; or that she is or was personally aquainted with important directors and other film personalities.  Excessive flamboyance conceals limited knowledge.

I attended 2 of Indorf's classes; as my mentor when I entered the School of the Arts Film Division, I can say she was always more than willing to be available to me to understand more fully what I wanted to explore, she also helped me personally.
A very phoney lady
A great instructor of film history and theory.

I took Ivo's summer course on Big Data, and it was a very good experience for me. I felt his teaching style was relaxed and enjoyable, and his attitude towards class was very positive. I look forward to learning from him again as I was very pleased with his work.
Mr. Djoubrailov is a really fun teacher who makes data analysis interesting. I would reccomend his courses!
Great class with a great instructor.

I struggled with this class because I didn't have a good foundation in Pre-Calc, and I felt like she could have been more helpful to me during exam times. However, her lectures were mostly clear (she does have an accent) and was very funny. I didn't do well, but someone who is up for the challenge of Columbia math will be fine.
Best option for calc at Columbia by far
She's pretty good I guess.
Organized, but does not really know what he is talking about.

Prof Nieh is the best and is my favorite CS professor at Columbia. I attended every lecture and each was structured and relevant to either OS concepts or the homeworks. No boring slides. He is engaging and uses very clever examples to simplify very complex concepts. He's genuinely a kind professor who cares about helping you learn. Underrated!!
really good prof
Brillant and enjoyable professor, but the content is okay, the exams are just unreasonable though

Good luck. Things are very different from what you might consider normal. Perhaps the instructor attended prestigious universities and underwent similar experiences, which could be why they want you to go through a comparable experience as well.

I really love this class. Professor Nieh is no nonsense about the material/grading but has a good dry sense of humor in lecture. He REALLY knows his stuff. Great course if you want learn the inner workings of computers and operating systems in detail. Extremely responsive on Ed and there are office hours every day of the week.

Prof. Jason is my favorite professor at Columbia University. He is caring, knowledgeable, and approachable. I  learned a great deal in his Operating Systems class. It prepared me well for the outside world and the world of SAAS startups. I greatly encourage everyone to take his class and get the help you greatly deserve as an Ivy League student.

I do not doubt that this professor knows what he is talking about, but being knowledgable on the material and being able to communicate it clearly to students are two seperate skillsets. This professor was not clear in his lectures nor was he helpful in his office hours when additional assistance was requested. I would avoid this professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Very difficult course and you will likely spend hours outside of class trying to understand the material. Professor Johannes knows his stuff and the notes are well designed (although typos). Grading was generous and questions were answered promptly.

excellent course for a sophisticated but not extremely rigorous introduction to stoc calculus.  Lower reviews due to lack of math and programming preparation / capabilities.  Lectures oscillate between math and real world examples (good or bad depending on preference).  would benefit with more discussion on "modern" vol models used in practice now
Hard to understand difficult stuff from his words.

Hard, poorly administered, poorly taught class. You'll learn a lot but suffer. Exams are fair and grading is generous.

Johannes is great nonetheless. Approachable and knowledgeable. He'll teach you invaluable intuition for financial markets. Maybe try his MBA classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Below reviews are not accurate. Sure his notes contain errors, but all are easily realized by a careful student. Dr. Johannes is a fine professor, and brings stochastic to calculus w/ several practical applications. If one struggles in this course it indicates more about the lack of their mathematical preparation than the quality of the professor.
It was a hellish experience.

His teaching style is not conducive to learning. There is a divide in his expectations and the skills of the students but he offers no constructive suggestions. He assigned lots of difficult work, often late and does not provide enough time to do them. His slides are littered with errors and lack detail so it is hard to follow. His exams are deadly
Difficult exam with no practice final. We also learned nothing from his lectures and note.
Writes illegibly
Give crystal clear lecture. Typical Chinese Teacher, give tons of homework
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This professor starts every class by saying things that create a lot of pressure—either talking about taking off points or insisting that every student must speak. The assignment requirements are also different from what's written in the syllabus, which makes every class frustrating.

LTC is an incredible opportunity to take a CSSW policy class from a true expert at the top of her field. Dr. Takamura brings a wealth of information and real-world experience to class. It's discussion-heavy, allowing for engaging, wide-ranging conversation. Warm, caring professor willing to provide career/networking support. Must read for class!

Dr. Takamura's fed policy class was one of the best classes I took at CSSW. It was engaging + rigorous, forcing you to work hard + drastically improve your writing + public speaking skills. Dr. Takamura gave insightful, helpful and detailed feedback on work. She is very available outside of class and gives excellent career advice. Take her class!

Dr. Takamura is one of CSSW's greatest assets. She comes with a wealth of knowledge and experience -- from high-level to nitty gritty. Skilled at translating information to her students. Warm, rigorous class environment; available outside of class; great feedback on assignments; provides ample networking opportunities. Not a class for slackers.
Heavy readings and discussions, unclear and even misleading guides for assignments, non-transparent grading criteria, the worst course I took in this school
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Had to drop after class #1. Never listed the text on the bookstore site and told us the textbook a week before classes started. She's clueless how to use Canvas yet treats her Tech Support very poorly. Gave unclear instructions on assignments and offers no clarification. Group assignments due the second week of class but she never assigned groups.
A true dolt.
Avoid at all costs!

This was the absolute worst professor that I have ever had. He digresses constantly and no one could grasp what he was talking about. I would avoid him at all costs. I had him for Judicial Pol and Logic of Collective choice. The logic class was the worst, he does not use a text book and the TA's didnt know anything.
This professor was horrible and his TA's were incompetent. He brags that his class will be hard and gives no one an A.
He is not the best teacher but I really dont like.

Prof.  Robertson is experienced and inspiring to students.  I, personally, dislike rating in terms of grading....this is preposterous.  Unfortunately, American students are hyper-focused on grades, not learning.  However, Dr. Robertson is a generous teacher, and impressive advocate.  His style of teaching is warm and receptive.

Professor Robertson is so passionate about what he teaches. He is genuinely involved and cares about the students. He is also super nice and helpful but especially very knowledge. I loved learning from him.

Dr. Robertson is one of the most amazing professors I've ever met. He is an incredibly intelligent person and he is really willing to share his knowledge to all students. I personally think the statement that he is unapproachable makes no sense- he's actually easy to reach out. Very thoughtful and open-minded person once you get to know him.

The grading criteria for assignments was unclear at times but our TA would always clarify. He had a habit of inviting people to ask questions and then saying that those questions should be asked privately/after class, but that could be because it was an online course. Overall, it was an okay class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Robertson is the real deal. He's an amazing professor with a wealth of knowledge. His classes definitely have a good amount of work, but you will learn a lot. I appreciate the space he creates for other opinions, and I also appreciate the push back he gives because I think it creates better social workers. Work hard you'll do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: A great professor. A lot of people might think him to be arrogant. That's probably because he likes challenge students in terms of their existing beliefs and thoughts. However, I think he is open-minded and respect people with different thoughts and backgrounds. I especially appreciate that. The requirements for paper is also clear and specific.

The Bad: a bit arrogant (but nowhere near the terrible Marian Riedel). The Good: I actually learned a lot in this class, which is more than I can say for many of the classes at this school. While he can be aloof, I think John Robertson is also a pretty good teacher and I appreciated his class making me work and challenging my beliefs.

Lectures are good. You have to do weekly assignments, but they're not a big deal. He can come across as a little arrogant, but I'm always glad to hear from a professor who will share their (well-supported) opinion.
Very Clear. Likes to challenge students to think from a different perspective, which I found really helpful and inspirational. Helpful. Has a sense of humor.
Definitely someone who needs to learn people skills first.  Does not respect students or their input.

Professor Robertson is an extremely intelligent, knowledgeable and effective teacher. He may not seem approachable in class, but if you get to know him, he's a very empathic and thoughtful person. I learned a lot from him about welfare policy and about myself.

Terrible professor. Avoid him if you can. He puts no effort into class, and so the two hours we spend there each week are useless. VERY confusing on assignments, and on grading as well. Worst of all, he is extremely pretentious, and very disrespectful to his students. I love the topic, and am really disappointed about this class.

Prof. Robertson is incredibly intelligent and I loved his lectures because I felt like I was really learning a lot. I can see what others are saying about the non-personability, but that didn't really bother me. I would recommend him because I did learn a lot however it does seem to take a lot to impress him.
He actually is approachable  You just have to approach him.  He is a very kind.  Once you approach him he'll be very helpful.

Knowledgeable alright, but I've had knowledgeable profs who are actually also personable. This prof is probably the one I dislike the most. He can't engage the class, people hardly participate, because he hasn't managed to create a good class atmosphere. He does not know how to reflect, respond, and relate to students' feedback. Preachy in lecture.
Very knowledgeable but very unapproachable and unclear about expectations for assignments.

I found Robertson to be very knowledgable but not at all personable. Whenever I had a question about an assignment, he made me feel like an idiot and refused to provide clarification. While I learned a lot in his class, I found him to be completely unapproachable. If policy isn't your cup of tea, I wouldn't suggest taking his class.

Prof assigns a LOT of reading and expects you to remember them in minute detail for his ridiculous pop quizzes (also the only policy prof who additionally quizzes you on the ny times). He is okay as a person, just wish he would be cognizant of everything else most SW students deal with and didn't set unrealistic expectations for reading.
one of the best professors in policy related classes
No Comments
No Comments
Class is interesting, and he adds real life through use of stories from the NYT.
Uses provably inaccurate information in course materials, exclusively pushes his personal agenda, lectures based on ideology and bigotry instead of historical fact.
Not accommodating, does not follow syllabus, goes off on rants about his personal beliefs, not a good class environment.
Unaccommodating and unreasonable professor.
Amazing teacher so nice
Did not learn a thing
Great professor. Very helpful and let's those who disagree with him state their opinion.
Excellent professor, skilled at engaging students in meaningful discussion, enriches discussion with well-founded insight and an impressive commitment to teaching
incredibly intelligent, and very knowledgeable and insightful.  Highly recommended.

Excellent professor by all standards. He has his opinions and isnt afaid to share them- and DOES allow others express different opinions. I think he is an excellent- esp. when he argues with facts and data rather than sheer emotional passions
Interesting narrative. Open to differing opinions. Engaging.
Brilliant, kind professor.  Fair and let's students speak
Inspirational and very humorous professor, take his calss once you'll know what the ratings are about.
Dr Kuras is brilliant. His lessons are fascinating, thought-provoking, inspiring and very profound. Immensely grateful for his classes.
a life-changing class. So inspirational. these courses he teaches are gifts.

Dr Kuras is a great professor. He makes you think, reflect upon your assumptions/bias and question everything you are taught. His explanations are very clear. Class topics are deep and engaging. 1000% recommended
Fascinating professor. Compelling and inspiring lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment:
Her leadership requires students to open up their mind and show weakness. Yet, it is a bit hard to do these when you don't feel genuine emotions from your instructor who doesn't seem to care that much. 

I do think she did a good job breaking down key leadership concepts and had profound experience. It is just she was a compelling leader for me.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Kirsti did an amazing job to guide us in our capstone project and provided valuable knowledge and feedback throughout the semester. We had to put in the work, but with Kirstis support paid off with a solid published report. I think any course with her as a lecturer would be interesting, especially since she has a wealth of knowledge/experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: I found Kirsti to be an excellent Capstone advisor: she is critical, but her criticism is constructive, specific and never arbitrary. Unlike many professors, she took the time to review our work product and give us helpful feedback as well as help us decide on a clear strategy for the research and analysis portion of the work.

Kirsti provided valuable guidance as an advisor to our team. I was disappointed that I didn't get the grade I wanted as I put in a lot of work. That aside, she was very thoughtful in handling the team, taking the time for healthy team dynamics and getting to know us. She skillfully balanced being an expert and giving us control of the project.
The professor supported us in writing a great capstone project that was published. Hard work. long hours.

Capstones are intensive,but Dr. Samuels made the project manageable. She has a wealth of knowledge and practical experience that she was willing and open to sharing. She took time to learn each of her students. She played up our strengths and assisted us in navigating through our challenges. Great teacher; Seasoned professional; Highly recommended.
Kirsti seemed disengaged and unavailable and provided little to no guidance to the team during the project. While she has an interesting CV, I did not feel like I learnt anything from her.

Kirsti was a great instructor. Her feedback on work was thoughtful and grounded in her wealth of experience in both peacebuilding and leadership development. She knew each student well, and was conscious of our group dynamics. (Note the bad reviews come from folks who declined to provide their grade. You get out what you put in.)

Kirsti is a wonderful professor and leader. She is very intentional, giving strategic guidance when needed, but also giving teams space to figure things out. Our team had an emergency abroad, and Kirsti went above and beyond to give us the tools to deal with the situation, provide support & show compassion. I would recommend her class to everyone.

Kirsti is a seasoned peace-building professional. She is knowledgeable of the theory and the practicalities of the field, which makes her a great tutor for a graduate course. I can only imagine that a course with her would be not only interesting but intellectually challenging.
She is super nice and knows her field!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I liked Professor Kostina and her class. It gave a good understanding of how Russian media has changed over the last thirty years since the USSR ended. There was a lot to cover, but her approach was organized and balanced. The class included weekly reading responses, a mid-term paper, and a final paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Kostina is kind, supportive, and well-organized. The lectures and discussions are structured and engaging. Watching Russian documentaries and movies was a great way to experience a culture I hadn't encountered before. She grades fairly and provides detailed feedback on the first draft of the final paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: A wonderful professor who is friendly, approachable, and flexible. She strikes a great balance between lectures and class discussions.Classroom environment is low-pressure and allows for different ideas and perspectives. Her structured and organized approach, along with the variety of readings and media genres, is a huge plus.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great class, enjoyed the readings and the lectures. The professor is super nice and approachable. Very knowledgable about literature, the immigration, politics, culture, theater, music, ballet, you name it - she can tell you tons about it! Just be open minded and ready for the discussions (hint - do your HW, read the assigned books!)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Inspirational--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Loved her! Very passionate about the topic and goes above and beyond to help the students and make it accessible! Highly recommended.
Highly recommended! An easy A, if you are ready to read, write weekly reading responses, and participate in class discussions. One class presentation and a final paper. Graded generously and fairly.

Prof. Katsnelson is one of my favorites! Her passion for the subject and deep knowledge about the books and authors are truly impressive. I especially loved how she brought the material to life with visits to émigré artists' galleries, which added such a unique perspective. She's both brilliant and kind, and I enjoyed every moment of her class!

I missed course evals this summer, so I had to find a way to make it known what a wonderful professor she was. Truly one of my favorite professors to date, she cares so much and her classes are so engaging. Her class was a real highlight in a hard time, and she is one of the sweetest people Ive ever met. And shes clearly quite brilliant!

As an international student, it was tough getting used to grad school, but her class helped me feel confident I could follow the material. The grading is very rigid. Students are not allowed to have their own ideas and must write 100% according to his logic. The average grade of the whole class is B-

Clearly she hates life and hates her students. She needs therapy and is evil. To achieve a grade distribution she asks questions on material she never taught rather than actually making hard, insightful questions.

Gem of a professor! Tough class, but you learn a lot

Orgo is like visiting the Titanic, and this is the tried-and-true steel sub. Sure, composites are lighter, but then again they don't always work.

talha is an AMAZING professor. i took orgo 1 lecture with her and she explains orgo in super easy way. her tests are very fair and gives a lot of practice. u need to go to lecture because she usually doesnt post her notes but her lectures are super clear and easy to understand that i always found myself WANTING to go to her class. best prof i had!

reviews.comment: Professor Holder is very warm and understanding. The workload is not unbearable and she was very knowledgable in the field. She is very experienced and I think she is a fantastic professor!

The lectures were a bit dry, but I appreciated Prof's effort to make the class engaging with videos and small group discussions. The assignment instructions were super unclear and Prof did not do a great job of clarifying or explaining. Grading wasn't extremely hard, but losing even 1 point impacted the grade a lot.
Professor Holder is not very engaging. The class was boring.  Your grade consists of one giant two-part literature review and an oral presentation.

She spoke down to graduate students like children & was often late, very sloppy and disorganized.Publicly shamed and demeaned students who dared to have an  opinion she didn't champion herself.She wanted opinions expressed to mirror her own, &would penalize when student papers didn't match her world own narrow world view. Discriminatory  practices.

She was nice, but read directly from the slides. She did not bring in any concrete examples from her work or that of others. She did not refer to readings, and did not present summaries of them at all, or draw from them in class. Do not take her!

She read directly from the slides and did not add any additional informaion or thought. there was no textbook and syllabus is disorganized. She was impatient when asked questions and treated us like elementary school students and not masters level students. However, she was very nice and well-intentioned. Just green.

I had her for HBSE B since I was doing the law minor and thought overall she was a good professor. Her assignments grew harder as the semester progressed but overall she's good.
Sara cares so much about students. It's exceptional and really made my SIPA experience. I fully recommend her classes and loved the academic freedom she gave us.

I really wanted to like prof Tjossem, but as others have alluded to, she is close minded and fails to support students equally. She has clear favorites. If you disagree with her worldview, she will dismiss you. She rambles. 95% of students in our lecture were multitasking thanks to her approach.

Favorite Professor I've had thus far as a graduate student here at Columbia. Passionate about the subject, highly knowledgeable, and invested in her students' success, she stands apart from other professors.

I would describe Sara with one word caustic. She seems all warm and emotive during lecture but if you offer up an idea which doesn't sync with her small worldview she will ignore you forever. Totally small minded.

Professor Tjossem is one of those rare professors that gets you excited about the subject. She has a deep understanding and passion for agriculture and sustainability, and it shows in her lectures. She speaks technically sometimes, but it's challenging and sparks tons of interest. Highly recommended!

Lecturer in the Environmental Science and Policy MPA program at SIPA. Dr. Tjossem teaches Environmental Policy, except "teach" might be too generous a verb.
